reforms.description: Colombia has broadened the eligibility criteria for R&D tax credits, allowing a wider range of firms to qualify, which is expected to stimulate business R&D spending significantly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform incentivizes increased R&D investment by firms, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the eligibility for R&D tax credits, impacting a broad range of firms.
reforms.source_quote: The eligibility of firms to qualify for R&D tax credits was broadened a few years ago and now attracts firms in a broad cross-section of sectors.

reforms.description: Colombia's Reindustrialisation Strategy, part of the National Development Plan 2022-2026, aims to transform the productive sector by supporting value chains crucial for national interests, focusing on sectors like renewable energy, agroindustry, health, and defense, while enhancing the country's mining policy to improve geological knowledge and support the transition to renewable energy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance productivity and economic diversification through targeted support in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been outlined in the National Development Plan but is yet to be fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in Colombia's approach to industrial policy and resource management.
reforms.source_quote: The Reindustrialisation Strategy is a broad-based policy to support the transformation of the productive sector to meet the challenges of climate change, accelerated technological change, and the changing geopolitical environment.

reforms.description: Colombia has introduced a virtual one-stop shop for starting a business, which integrates administrative procedures and aims to reduce the burden on new startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative simplifies the process of starting a business, encouraging entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The one-stop shop is operational and assisting new businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is more of an administrative improvement rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Colombia introduced a virtual one-stop shop after 2018, resulting in a significant improvement in the burden of administrative requirements on new start-ups.

reforms.description: Colombia is evaluating and expanding the supplier development project introduced by Colombia Productiva in 2023 to enhance linkages between foreign and local firms, while also developing an alternative credit scoring methodology that utilizes public databases to enhance financial inclusion for previously unbanked populations.
reforms.package_name: Knowledge Transfer and Financial Inclusion Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to improve productivity and access to finance, fostering a more inclusive economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives are in the planning and evaluation stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but do not represent a major overhaul of existing systems.
reforms.source_quote: Colombia could learn from Costa Rica’s experience, for example by evaluating and expanding the supplier development project introduced by Colombia Productiva in 2023.

reforms.description: The CREO programme aims to increase credit access for vulnerable households through subsidized microcredit lines backed by public guarantees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to credit, the programme supports entrepreneurship and the growth of small businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been proposed and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant for vulnerable populations but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s flagship programme CREO goes in this direction. It consists of a subsidised micro credit line of up to around USD 500 backed by a public guarantee.

reforms.description: The government is implementing comprehensive training programmes for displaced workers to equip them with skills for new job opportunities in sectors related to the green transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The training programmes aim to enhance the skill set of the workforce, supporting the transition to a greener economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes are in the planning stages and have not yet been rolled out.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for workforce development, the reform is not a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: Deploying comprehensive training programmes for displaced workers, equipping them with the necessary skills to access job opportunities in new sectors, is key to minimise the social impact of potential job displacements.
